title: Continuous Scatterplot and Image Moments for Time-Varying Bivariate Field Analysis of Electronic Structure Evolution
authorText: Mohit Sharma, Talha Bin Masood, Nanna Holmgaard List, Ingrid Hotz, and Vijay Natarajan
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: This work presents a structured approach for feature-directed visual exploration of time-varying bivariate fields using continuous scatterplots and image moment descriptors, with applications to electronic structure evolution after photoexcitation.
bibtex: @article{sharma2025cspmoments, title={Continuous Scatterplot and Image Moments for Time-Varying Bivariate Field Analysis of Electronic Structure Evolution}, author={Sharma, Mohit and Masood, Talha Bin and List, Nanna Holmgaard and Hotz, Ingrid and Natarajan, Vijay}, journal={IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics}, year={2025}}

title: Continuous Scatterplot Operators for Bivariate Analysis and Study of Electronic Transitions
authorText: Mohit Sharma, Talha Bin Masood, Signe S. Thygesen, Mathieu Linares, Ingrid Hotz, and Vijay Natarajan
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: This paper introduces continuous scatterplot operators for the analysis of bivariate fields and demonstrates their use in studying electronic transitions, donor-acceptor behavior, and molecular electronic structure.
bibtex: @article{sharma2024cspoperators, title={Continuous Scatterplot Operators for Bivariate Analysis and Study of Electronic Transitions}, author={Sharma, Mohit and Masood, Talha Bin and Thygesen, Signe S. and Linares, Mathieu and Hotz, Ingrid and Natarajan, Vijay}, journal={IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics}, year={2024}}

title: Jacobi Set Simplification for Tracking Topological Features in Time-Varying Scalar Fields
authorText: Dhruv Meduri, Mohit Sharma, and Vijay Natarajan
abstract: This work simplifies Jacobi sets for time-varying scalar fields to reduce visual clutter and improve the analysis of topological feature tracks.
bibtex: @article{meduri2024jacobi, title={Jacobi Set Simplification for Tracking Topological Features in Time-Varying Scalar Fields}, author={Meduri, Dhruv and Sharma, Mohit and Natarajan, Vijay}, journal={The Visual Computer}, year={2024}}

title: Topology-Aware Volume Fusion for Spectral Computed Tomography via Histograms and Extremum Graph
authorText: Mohit Sharma, Emma Nilsson, Martin Falk, Talha Bin Masood, Lee Jollans, Anders Persson, Tino Ebbers, and Ingrid Hotz
abstract: This paper proposes a topology-aware method for fusing spectral computed tomography data into a representative volume using histograms and extremum graphs while preserving salient anatomical structures.
bibtex: @inproceedings{sharma2025volumefusion, title={Topology-Aware Volume Fusion for Spectral Computed Tomography via Histograms and Extremum Graph}, author={Sharma, Mohit and Nilsson, Emma and Falk, Martin and Masood, Talha Bin and Jollans, Lee and Persson, Anders and Ebbers, Tino and Hotz, Ingrid}, booktitle={TopoInVis}, year={2025}}

title: Topology-Guided Volume Fusion for Multi-Energy Spectral Computed Tomography
authorText: Mohit Sharma, Emma Nilsson, Martin Falk, Talha Bin Masood, Lee Jollans, Anders Persson, Tino Ebbers, and Ingrid Hotz
venue: Eurographics Workshop on Visual Computing for Biology and Medicine (VCBM) Poster
abstract: This poster presents topology-guided volume fusion for multi-energy spectral computed tomography, using topological and histogram-based information to preserve important anatomical structures while producing a representative fused volume.
bibtex: @misc{sharma2025vcbmposter, title={Topology-Guided Volume Fusion for Multi-Energy Spectral Computed Tomography}, author={Sharma, Mohit and Nilsson, Emma and Falk, Martin and Masood, Talha Bin and Jollans, Lee and Persson, Anders and Ebbers, Tino and Hotz, Ingrid}, howpublished={VCBM Poster}, year={2025}}

title: Jacobi Set Driven Search for Flexible Fiber Surface Extraction
abstract: A Jacobi-set-driven approach for flexible fiber surface extraction in bivariate fields.
bibtex: @inproceedings{sharma2022fiber, title={Jacobi Set Driven Search for Flexible Fiber Surface Extraction}, author={Sharma, Mohit and Natarajan, Vijay}, booktitle={TopoInVis}, year={2022}}

title: Segmentation Driven Peeling for Visual Analysis of Electronic Transitions
authorText: Mohit Sharma, Talha Bin Masood, Signe S. Thygesen, Mathieu Linares, Ingrid Hotz, and Vijay Natarajan
abstract: This work presents a segmentation-driven peeling method for visual analysis of electronic transitions using bivariate fields derived from electron density data.

title: On-Demand Augmentation of Contour Trees
abstract: An on-demand approach for augmenting contour trees to support flexible topological analysis of scalar fields.
